On BAGEL, it achieves a $+3.5\%$ absolute gain on MMMU and improves GenEval image generation performance from $82\%$ to $85\%$. Code and models are publicly released.

AI.tldr: A self-evolving training framework for unified LMMs that improves both visual understanding and image generation using only unlabeled images, without human annotations or external rewards, achieving consistent gains across multiple architectures and benchmarks.
AI.motivation: Current unified LMMs for visual understanding and image generation require curated post-training supervision (human annotations, preference labels, or external reward models). The authors ask whether a unified LMM can improve both abilities autonomously using only unlabeled images, eliminating the need for expensive human-curated supervision.
AI.method: A self-evolving training framework with three internal roles: Proposer (generates visual questions), Solver (answers and evaluates them), and Generator (synthesizes images). Training uses self-derived consistency signals. Key innovations: (1) Solver Token Entropy (STE) for stable difficulty signals using token-level uncertainty; (2) multi-scale internal evaluation for image generation combining QA fidelity scoring with cycle-consistent captioning; (3) solver-mediated coupling linking understanding quality to generation assessment. The framework works across BLIP3o, BAGEL, and VARGPT-v1.1 architectures.
AI.result: Consistent improvements across 8 understanding metrics. On BAGEL: +3.5% absolute gain on MMMU, GenEval improved from 82% to 85%. Framework generalizes across diffusion-based, rectified-flow, and autoregressive architectures.
AI.conclusion: A unified LMM can autonomously improve both visual understanding and image generation using only unlabeled images through self-evolving training with consistency signals, eliminating the need for curated post-training supervision.

title: DanceOPD: On-Policy Generative Field Distillation
comment: Technical Report; 39 pages, 13 figures, 9 tables; Project Page at https://danceopd.github.io/
summary: Modern image generation demands a single model that unifies diverse capabilities, including text-to-image (T2I), local editing, and global editing. However, these capabilities are rarely naturally aligned and often conflict. For instance, editing tends to degrade T2I performance, while global and local editing interfere with each other. Consequently, effectively composing these capabilities has become a central challenge for image generation model training. To tackle this, we introduce DanceOPD, an on-policy generative field distillation framework for flow-matching models that routes each sample to one capability field, queries one low-noise student-induced state, and trains with a simple velocity MSE objective. With each capability source defined as a velocity field over the shared flow state space, the student learns from fields queried on its own rollout states to compose expert capabilities. This formulation also absorbs operator-defined fields such as classifier-free guidance. Comprehensive experiments on T2I, editing, realism-field absorption, and CFG absorption show that our approach improves multi-capability composition, strengthening target capabilities while preserving anchor generation quality. We believe this work establishes a practical route for generative field distillation in flow-matching models.

AI.tldr: DanceOPD introduces an on-policy generative field distillation framework for flow-matching models that routes samples to capability-specific fields, enabling effective composition of T2I, local editing, and global editing without performance degradation.
AI.motivation: Modern image generation requires a single model unifying T2I, local editing, and global editing, but these capabilities naturally conflict (e.g., editing degrades T2I, global and local editing interfere), making effective composition a central challenge.
AI.method: DanceOPD is an on-policy generative field distillation framework for flow-matching models that: (1) routes each sample to one capability field, (2) queries one low-noise student-induced state, and (3) trains with a simple velocity MSE objective. Each capability source is a velocity field over the shared flow state space; the student learns from fields queried on its own rollout states to compose expert capabilities, also absorbing operator-defined fields like CFG.
AI.result: Comprehensive experiments on T2I, editing, realism-field absorption, and CFG absorption show improved multi-capability composition, strengthening target capabilities while preserving anchor generation quality.
AI.conclusion: DanceOPD establishes a practical route for generative field distillation in flow-matching models, enabling effective multi-capability composition.
